summary: sensivity@2.5.65 ships eval'd obfuscated payload with persistence, process masquerade, and host fingerprint exfiltration
details: Package main entry launcher.js loads a 161KB RC4/obfuscator.io-encoded blob (server.obf.js) and executes it via eval(fs.readFileSync(...)) on every require. The decoded blob constructs a stable hardware identifier by combining the Windows registry MachineGuid (read via PowerShell Get-ItemProperty HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Cryptography), os.hostname(), and the C: volume serial, SHA-256s them, and POSTs {key, hwid, nonce, app:'sensivity', version} to a hardcoded remote endpoint whose hostname is string-split for concealment (begins with 'https://iz'). launcher.js additionally calls ensureAutostart() which writes HKCU\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Run\OneDriveUpdate pointing wscript.exe at a bundled OneDrive.Standalone.Updater.vbs to silently relaunch the package at every Windows login, masquerading as a Microsoft OneDrive updater. The launcher also sets process.title='Runtime Broker' (a real Windows OS process name) and monkey-patches console.log/warn/error to rewrite occurrences of 'sens.node' to 'program module' and swallow 'program module loaded' lines — active evasion designed to hide the bundled 2MB sens.node native addon from Task Manager and runtime logs. Decoded strings ('FiveM process not found', 'getPlayerList', 'Vehicle list', 'performAction') and PowerShell scans for the FiveM game window confirm the actual function is FiveM game-cheating software, not the 'Sensivity Control Panel' the package metadata advertises. Multiple independent attacker-benefit mechanisms are present: remote code execution via eval of opaque content at load time, exfiltration of stable host identifiers to an obfuscated remote endpoint, and a Microsoft-impersonating Run-key persistence implant.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
